title: Unikraft Blog
description: A fast, secure and open-source Unikernel Development Kit.

items.title: Unikraft Filesystem Stack
items.summary: This blog post provides a technical overview of the new VFS stack introduced in Unikraft 0.20.0.

items.title: Unikraft releases v0.20.0 (Kiviuq)
items.summary: This release comes with the much anticipated redesign and implementation of VFS, which brings enhanced performance, better maintainability, and improved compatibility with modern applications.

items.title: Unikraft releases v0.19.1 (Pan)
items.summary: This release contains fixes and improvements to Unikraft.

items.title: Unikraft releases v0.19.0 (Pan)
items.summary: This release contains features and improvements to Unikraft.

items.title: Five Unikraft Projects Selected at Google Summer of Code 2025
items.summary: It is our pleasure to announce that 5 Unikraft projects are part of Google Summer of Code 2025 (GSoC'25).

items.title: Multiprocess support on Unikraft
items.summary: This blog post provides technical details on the implementation of multiprocess support introduced in Unikraft 0.19.0.

items.title: Unikraft releases v0.18.0 (Helene)
items.summary: This release contains features and improvements to Unikraft.

items.title: Multiboot2 Support in Unikraft
items.summary: In this blog post, I discuss the final weeks of my GSoC project, highlighting the process of testing Nuktiboot2 support on the helloworld and nginx applications, and reflecting on the progress made in enhancing the boot process.

items.title: GSoC'24: UEFI Graphics Output Protocol Support in Unikraft, Part IV
items.summary: This is the fourth post in a series of posts where I talk about my progress with the project.

items.title: GSoC'24: Benchmarking mimalloc
items.summary:
Different applications have different memory usage patterns and perform better
when using dedicated memory allocators. Right now, the buddy allocator is the only
available memory allocator used in Unikraft, so this GSoC project aims to 
port more memory allocators to it, starting with mimalloc.

items.title: GSoC'24: UEFI Graphics Output Protocol Support in Unikraft, Part III
items.summary: This is the third post in a series of posts where I talk about my progress with the project.

items.title: GSoC'24: Linux x86 Boot Protocol Support
items.summary: The Linux boot protocol plays an important role in the initialization of the Linux operating system, emphasizing the importance of system optimization and scalability.

items.title: Multiboot2 Support in Unikraft
items.summary: In this blog post, I go over the debugging process and the progress made in the implementation of Multiboot2 support these past three weeks of GSoC.

items.title: GSoC'24: Debugging the Multithreaded Memory Allocation Interface
items.summary:
Different applications have different memory usage patterns and perform better
when using dedicated memory allocators. Right now, the buddy allocator is the only
available memory allocator used in Unikraft, so this GSoC project aims to 
port more memory allocators to it, starting with mimalloc.

items.title: GSoC'24: Testing the mimalloc Memory Allocator on Unikraft
items.summary:
Different applications have different memory usage patterns and perform better
when using dedicated memory allocators. Right now, the buddy allocator is the only
available memory allocator used in Unikraft, so this GSoC project aims to 
port more memory allocators to it, starting with mimalloc.

items.title: Multiboot2 Support in Unikraft
items.summary: In my previous blog post, I focused on the first steps of the project: understanding the Multiboot2 protocol and preparing the development environment. Over the following three weeks, I progressed to implementing the necessary changes/additions and then testing the code.

items.title: GSoC'24: UEFI Graphics Output Protocol Support in Unikraft, Part II
items.summary: This is the second post in a series of posts where I talk about my progress with the project.

items.title: GSoC'24: Linux x86 Boot Protocol Support
items.summary: The Linux boot protocol plays an important role in the initialization of the Linux operating system, emphasizing the importance of system optimization and scalability.

items.title: GSoC'24: Supporting User-provided, Long-lived Environmental Variables for Unikraft Builds
items.summary: The goal of this project is to improve Unikraft's tooling, more especially, the `kraft` command-line tool, so that it can dynamically inject user-supplied variables into the build system.

items.title: GSoC'24: UEFI Graphics Output Protocol Support in Unikraft
items.summary: This project aims to add support for the modern UEFI GOP interface in Unikraft.

items.title: GSoC'24: Linux x86 Boot Protocol Support
items.summary: The Linux boot protocol plays an important role in the initialization of the Linux operating system, emphasizing the importance of system optimization and scalability.

items.title: Multiboot2 Support in Unikraft
items.summary: Multiboot2, an enhanced version of Multiboot, solves some of the limitations of the original protocol, such as the lack of support for 64-bit UEFI systems. It also provides a more accurate and detailed communication during the bootstrapping process and offers a standardized tag system for easier configuration.

items.title: GSoC'24: Porting the mimalloc Memory Allocator to Unikraft
items.summary:
Different applications have different memory usage patterns and perform better
when using dedicated memory allocators. Right now, the buddy allocator is the only
available memory allocator used in Unikraft, so this GSoC project aims to 
port more memory allocators to it, starting with mimalloc.

items.title: Unikraft releases v0.17.0 (Calypso)
items.summary: This release contains features and improvements to Unikraft.

items.title: Five Unikraft Projects Selected at Google Summer of Code 2024
items.summary: It is our pleasure to announce that 5 Unikraft projects are part of Google Summer of Code 2024 (GSoC'24).

items.title: Unikraft releases v0.16.3 (Telesto)
items.summary: This release contains fixes, documentation and application updates.

items.title: Unikraft releases v0.16.2 (Telesto)
items.summary: This release contains fixes primarily related to platform and application support.

items.title: Unikraft releases v0.16.1 (Telesto)
items.summary: This release contains both important and breaking changes related to the KConfig and syntax when statically specifying network interface information as well as the list of fstab (automount) entries at the kernel command-line.

items.title: Unikraft releases v0.16.0 (Telesto)
items.summary:
This release is the result of extensive hard work during the last months in
the entire community.

items.title: KraftKit v0.7.0 Release
items.summary: A massive update to a unikernel developer's best friend.

items.title: Unikraft releases v0.15.0 (Pandora)
items.summary:
This release is the result of extensive hard work during the last months in
the entire community.

items.title: Unikraft releases v0.14.0 (Prometheus)
items.summary:
This release is the result of extensive hard work during the last months in
the entire community.

items.title: Five Unikraft Projects Selected at Google Summer of Code 2023
items.summary:
It is our pleasure to announce that five Unikraft projects are part of Google
Summer of Code 2023 (GSoC'23).

items.title: GSoC'23: Arm CCA Support for Unikraft (Part 1)
items.summary:
This project  aims to bring Arm Confidential Computing Architecture (CCA)
feature to Unikraft.

items.title: GSoC'23: Enhancing VS Code Developer Experience
items.summary:
The VS Code Extension for Unikraft enables developers to quickly and
painlessly build unikernels from the VS Code IDE.

items.title: Packaging Pre-built Micro-libraries for Faster and More Secure Builds
items.summary: Progress on my GSoC'23 project.

items.title: re:Arch Unikraft
items.summary:
The goal of this GSoC'23 project is to optimize and clearly define drivers and 
platform support.

items.title: Expanding Binary Compatibility Mode (Part 1)
items.summary:
This project aims to enhance the binary compatibility of Unikraft. Including 
enabling the libc dynamic library to directly access kernel functions, 
implementing VDSO,  improving application compatibility and enhancing CI/CD.

items.title: Tales of Open Source: vfscore
items.summary:
The beauty of open source is the immediate availability of source code that 
you can use to create or improve your software. Of course, this relies on
compatible licensing...

items.title: Unikraft releases v0.13.0 (Atlas)
items.summary: In this blog post, we describe some of the new features available in Unikraft.

items.title: Unikraft releases v0.12.0 (Epimetheus)
items.summary:
With this relese, we aim to bring more stability to the Musl support
integrated in 0.11, but also come with a lot of exciting features.

items.title: Building the Future of Security and Isolation with Unikraft on Morello!
items.summary:
I have recently ported a proof-of-concept version of Unikraft to run 
bare metal on the Morello machine, and I am in the process of 
exploring the cool security properties that combining CHERI with a 
modern unikernel such as Unikraft can bring.

items.title: Unikraft releases v0.11.0 (Janus)
items.summary:
This release is the result of around 3 months of hard work in the entire 
community, with a focus on integrating the long awaited musl support.

items.title: Unikraft Munich Hackathon '22
items.summary:
Last weekend, we had yet another successful hackathon with plenty of 
hard work.  But this one was special since it was organized in München.

items.title: Unikraft vs. UKL: What's the Difference?
items.summary:
You may have seen recent news about a set of patches] hitting the 
Linux kernel mailing list for Unikernel Linux (UKL), "... a research 
project aimed at integrating application-specific optimizations to the 
Linux kernel.

items.title: Unikraft Community Meet-up '22
items.summary:
We are excited to tell you about the first in-person Unikraft community meet-
up, a long awaited event which was painfully postponed by the pandemic.

items.title: Unikraft Reaches 1K+ GitHub Stars! (and 500 Discord Users)
items.summary:
We're ecstatic to announce that a week ago Unikraft reached 1K stars 
on Github on its main repository!

items.title: Unikraft Hacktoberfest '22
items.summary:
As tradition dictates, if it's Saturday then it is definitely another
hackathon we are proud to host at Unikraft. Even so, the last one was a bit
special.

items.title: Radix-Tree: Internal data structures for SGX EPC Page Management
items.summary:
In this article, we explore Radix tree's in the context of SGC EPC page
management.

items.title: Adding I/O APIC support
items.summary:
This blog describes the ongoing work on the I/O APIC integration in unikraft.
Currently Unikraft is using the traditional 8259 PIC interrupt controller.

items.title: Unikraft releases v0.10.0 (Phoebe)
items.summary:
We're very excited to announce the latest edition of Unikraft, v0.10.0
(Phoebe), and to show off many of the things the community has been working on
over the last two months.

items.title: Shadow Stack (Part 3)
items.summary:
This third blog post presents the efforts that were made in the 
direction of testing and perfecting complex apps (such as `SQLite`, 
`redis` and `nginx`) on `AArch64` using `gcc`, `clang` and `gcc-12` as 
compilers.

items.title: Leveraging Intel SGX in Unikraft: Challenges and Recent progress
items.summary:
In this post, we will take an in-depth investigation of how SGX supported is
implemented in Linux, and what we need to implement in Unikraft to achieve the
same object.

items.title: Shadow Stack (Part 2)
items.summary:
While the previous blog post described the first steps took into the direction
of familiarizing myself with Unikraft and an initial attempt to using
`clang`'s ShadowCallStack, in this post, we will take a look into some
implementations that were tried in the meantime.

items.title: Adding SMP support
items.summary:
The previous blog on the SMP support explained different 
synchronization primitives for the unikraft.  As a part of the next 
step, I started exploring some of the lockless data structures. There 
has been an extensive amount of research to optimize this lockless 
data structure.

items.title: Adding SMP support
items.summary:
This project aims to add the SMP safe synchronization premitives as well as
remove the race conditions in the kernel.

items.title: Shadow Stack (Part 1)
items.summary:
While Unikraft provides great security advantages through strong 
cross-application isolation, traditional means of securing one's 
application shouldn't be overlooked.

items.title: Unikraft releases v0.9.0 (Hyperion)
items.summary:
We're excited to announce Unikraft v0.9.0 (Hyperion) and to show off 
many of the things the community has been working on over the last two 
months.

items.title: Improving security and isolation of Unikraft with Intel SGX
items.summary:
Trusted execution environment (TEE), especially Intel SGX, is a 
popular and powerful tool to provide hardware-based isolation for 
highly sensitive code and data.

items.title: Three Unikraft projects selected at Google Summer of Code 2022
items.summary:
We're excited to announce that three Unikraft projects will be part of 
Google Summer of Code, with three students funded during the summer of 
2022.

items.title: Unikraft accepted as Google Summer of Code Organization!
items.summary:
We are extremely proud to have been accepted as an organization for Google
Summer of Code 2022 (GSoC'22)

items.title: Interested in Security for Unikraft?
items.summary:
We have finally gotten around to documenting all of the security features that
make Unikraft and exciting platform for those interested in security work and
secure deployments.

items.title: Unikraft releases v0.8.0 (Enceladus)
items.summary:
We're excited to announce Unikraft v0.8.0 (Enceladus) and to show off many of
the things the community has been working on over the last two months.

items.title: Unikraft and more at ASPLOS’22
items.summary:
Unikraft will appear at the International Conference on Architectural Support
for Programming Languages and Operating Systems (ASPLOS'22) with both a paper
and a workshop.

items.title: Kicking off 2022 with a new site and release at FOSDEM!
items.summary:
As the new year begins, we're excited to show off many of the things the
community has been working over the last two months.

items.title: Unikraft: Fast, Specialized Unikernels the Easy Way
items.summary:
The open source Unikraft project is proud to announce that its paper titled
"Unikraft: Fast, Specialized Unikernels the Easy Way" has not only been
accepted at Eurosys.

items.title: Unikraft: Building Powerful Unikernels Has Never Been Easier!
items.summary:
Two years ago, the Xen Project introduced Unikraft as an incubation project.
Over the past two years, the Unikraft project has seen some great momentum.

items.title: Xen Project Celebrates Unikraft Unikernel Project's One Year Anniversary
items.summary:
It has been one year since the Xen Project introduced Unikraft as an incubator
project. In that time, the team has made great strides in simplifying the
process of building unikernels through a unified and customizable code base.

items.title: Unleasing the Power of Unikernels
items.summary:
The team at NEC Laboratories Europe spent quite a bit of time over the last
few years developing unikernels (specialized virtual machine images targeting
specific applications).
